# Phase 1 Coverage Report — the λ Locking/Conviction Index

**Project:** AFA 2027 QTM Crypto. **Phase:** 1 (λ channels).
**Sample:** 2015-08-31 → 2026-05-31 (130 month-ends); λ computed on `status='observed'`
asset-months only.
**Generated by:** `04_code/phase1_build_identity_map.py`, `phase1_channel1_eth_staking.py`,
`phase1_channel1_evm_locks.py`, `phase1_channel3_voting.py`, `phase1_assemble_lambda.py`.
**Source of truth for *why*:** `04_code/DATA_DECISIONS_LOG.md` Entries 21–27.

> **Headline.** λ is built from *canonical* sources per the project directive — on-chain
> event logs (Etherscan V2) for staking/locking and Snapshot's own API for voting, **not**
> from an aggregator. The free-data landscape (no archive state, paywalled staking
> dashboards) means λ is **dense for governance tokens and thin for coins** in this first
> pass: 51 governance tokens get a voting-based λ, 6 vote-escrow tokens get a **two-channel**
> λ (locking + voting), and ETH (the flagship coin) is reconstructable from the beacon
> deposit contract (method validated; full series is a long cached run). The single largest
> gap — holding-duration (Channel 2) and non-EVM native-coin staking — gates early-sample
> and coin-side λ, exactly as the spec anticipated.

---

## 1. Method (one paragraph)

For each observed asset-month we compute up to three conviction channels, **z-score each
channel within its monthly cross-section** (using only the observed assets that actually
have that channel that month, and only where ≥2 assets share it so a standard deviation
exists), and set **λ = the equal-weighted mean of the available standardized channels**.
Missing channels are **never imputed**; we record `n_channels` and which channels
contributed. Everything joins on `cmc_id`. Output: `03_data/phase1/lambda_panel.csv`.

## 2. Source verification (live, 2026-06-23) — what works, what is paywalled

Per spec §3 step 3, every source was probed before use (full detail: Entry 21).

| Source | Role | Status |
|---|---|---|
| **Snapshot GraphQL** (`hub.snapshot.org`) | Channel 3 voting (canonical off-chain vote store) | ✅ free, keyless |
| **Etherscan V2** (`api.etherscan.io/v2`) | Channel 1 on-chain logs (60+ EVM chains, one key) | ✅ works with free key (V1 dead) |
| **DeFiLlama** (`api.llama.fi`) | registry only (cmc_id → contract/space); **not** a data source | ✅ free, keyless |
| **CoinMarketCap** historical | circulating-supply denominator (Phase 0 backbone) | ✅ (reused) |
| **beaconcha.in** | ETH staking dashboard | ❌ now 401 (paywalled) → replaced by deposit-contract logs |
| **Boardroom / Tally** | voting | ❌ now 401/keyed → Snapshot used instead |
| free public RPCs (cloudflare/ankr/publicnode/llama/1rpc) | historical state | ❌ archive blocked on all |
| Etherscan free `eth_call` at past block | historical balanceOf | ❌ **silently latest-only** → forced event-log reconstruction |

The last two rows are the binding constraint: **no free archive state exists**, so every
historical Channel-1 number is reconstructed from immutable event logs, not state reads.

## 3. Asset → on-chain-identity map (precursor)

`phase1_build_identity_map.py` joins the 1,939 in-universe assets to DeFiLlama's protocol
registry on `cmcId`. Coverage is thin — DeFiLlama's `cmcId` is sparsely populated:
241/1,939 matched, 206 with a token address, **35 with a Snapshot space**. This thinness is
*why* the voting space map is hand-extended and the lock set is curated (below). Enriching
the map from CMC's own `detail.platforms[]` (cleaner per-asset contract data) is the obvious
way to widen Channel 1 later (Entry 22).

## 4. Channel-by-channel coverage

### 4.1 Channel 1 — staking / locking ratio (canonical on-chain)

**(a) ETH native staking** — reconstructed from the Beacon Chain deposit contract's
`DepositEvent` logs (cumulative deposited ETH ÷ circulating), respecting
`staking_start=2020-12-01` (NaN before; PoW). Method **validated** against public levels
(2.17M ETH staked at 2020-12, 5.2M at 2021-05). **Caveat:** the deposit contract only
receives ether, so post-Shapella (2023-04) the cumulative figure overstates net active
stake (it is a monotone upper-envelope conviction proxy). The full 2020-12→2026-05 series
is a ~2.5M-event cached run; it is resumable via re-running the script (Entry 23).

**(b) EVM vote-escrow / staking tokens** — locked supply reconstructed from base-token
Transfer logs into/out of the escrow contract, for 6 curated high-confidence escrows.
**All 6 validate to within <3% of the live on-chain balance** (Entry 26):

| token | mechanism | months | first | latest locking ratio | recon vs live |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CRV | veCRV vote-escrow | 69 | 2020-08 | **0.565** | 0.1% |
| CVX | vlCVX vote-lock | 56 | 2021-10 | **0.476** | 0.3% |
| FXS | veFXS vote-escrow | 54 | 2021-12 | **0.179** | 0.4% |
| AAVE | stkAAVE safety-module | 67 | 2020-10 | **0.137** | 2.3% |
| SUSHI | xSUSHI fee-share stake | 69 | 2020-08 | **0.064** | 0.9% |
| YFI | veYFI vote-escrow | 70 | 2020-07 | **0.043** | 0.0% |

Excluded by design (documented, not silently proxied): **veBAL** (locks an 80/20 BPT, not
BAL) and **SNX** (collateral C-ratio system). CVX pre-2022 is understated (locks were in the
V1 locker; the tracked address is CvxLockerV2) — recent values are exact.

**Channel-1 gaps:** non-EVM native PoS coins (Solana, Cosmos, Cardano, Tron, XRPL, …) are
unreachable with an EVM key — the bulk of the 633 coins have **no** staking channel this
pass. Widening needs per-chain explorers or a paid archive tier.

### 4.2 Channel 2 — holding duration / coin-age: **NOT BUILT (documented gap)**

A credible coin-age / HODL-wave series needs full per-chain transfer/UTXO history or a paid
fundamentals API (Glassnode/CoinMetrics/Artemis); no free source serves it across the
panel's chains (Entry 24). Per the spec's operating principle we **flag the gap rather than
ship an unsupportable proxy**. The `ch2_holding` column exists in the schema and is always
NaN. Because §3.2 notes this would be the *only* channel for pre-2020 coins, its absence is
exactly what leaves early-sample coins without a λ.

### 4.3 Channel 3 — voting engagement (canonical, Snapshot)

Token-weighted turnout = mean(`scores_total`) ÷ circulating supply, per month a proposal's
voting **ends**. Space map = DeFiLlama `governanceID` (35) ∪ 27 curated, name-verified major
DAOs keyed by explicit `cmc_id` (56 spaces; Entry 25). A **token-weight validity guard**
nulls 1-person-1-vote/ticket spaces (caught snxgov, enzyme, ilvgov). Result: **55 DAOs with
voting data, 51 with a valid token-weighted turnout, 1,178 valid asset-months, 2020-07 →
2026-06.** On-chain-only DAOs (MKR, LQTY, PENDLE, RUNE, PERP, WLD, ONDO, ENA, Compound
Governor, …) are not on Snapshot → a documented gap requiring on-chain Governor VoteCast
reconstruction later.

## 5. The λ index — coverage

`lambda_panel.csv`: **1,326 asset-months, 52 distinct assets, 2020-08 → 2026-05.**

**By number of contributing channels:**

| n_channels | asset-months | which |
|---|---|---|
| 2 (staking+voting) | 253 | the 6 vote-escrow tokens: CRV, CVX, FXS, SUSHI, AAVE, YFI |
| 1 | 1,073 | voting-only 924 · staking/locking-only 149 (incl. ETH's 18 months) |

**λ asset-months by year × n_channels:**

| year | 1-channel | 2-channel | total |
|---|---|---|---|
| 2020 | 15 | 7 | 22 |
| 2021 | 137 | 31 | 168 |
| 2022 | 222 | 53 | 275 |
| 2023 | 254 | 47 | 301 |
| 2024 | 215 | 53 | 268 |
| 2025 | 176 | 47 | 223 |
| 2026 | 54 | 15 | 69 |

**Distinct assets with any λ, by class:** token 43 · coin 5 · other 4. (ETH's staking λ
currently spans 2020-12→2022-05, the 18 reconstructed months; it extends as the series
completes.) The token dominance
is structural: Channel 3 (voting) and the vote-escrow Channel 1 are governance-token
phenomena; coins reach λ only through native staking (ETH, plus the few mineable/PoS coins
that happen to have a Snapshot space). **There is currently no λ for any pre-2020
asset-month and almost none for pure coins** — both follow directly from the Channel-2 gap
and the non-EVM staking gap.

## 6. What this gates (hypothesis testability)

- **H1b (governance tokens)** — *best supported.* 51 tokens get a voting λ from ~2020-08;
  the 6 vote-escrow tokens get the two-channel (locking + voting-weighted) λ that H1b's
  second sentence specifically calls for. Density is real from 2021 onward.
- **H1a (coins)** — *weak this pass.* Only ETH (and a handful of staking-capable coins) can
  get a Channel-1 λ; the 600+ non-EVM PoS coins and all pre-2020 coin-months have no channel.
  H1a needs either the full ETH series + more native-staking sources, or Channel 2.
- **H2 / H3 (NVT_GL conditioning, quadrant)** — depend on Phase 2 (NVT_GL) and on λ density;
  testable mainly within the governance-token cross-section from ~2021.

## 7. Open questions / recommendations before Phase 2

1. **Finish the ETH staking series** (resume `phase1_channel1_eth_staking.py`; ~2hr cached
   run) so H1a has its flagship coin; decide whether to net out post-Shapella withdrawals
   (needs a consensus-layer source) or keep the cumulative-deposit upper envelope.
2. **Channel 2 is the highest-value missing piece** — a paid fundamentals API (Glassnode/
   CoinMetrics) or a BTC/ETH full-node indexer would unlock early-sample coin λ. Decide
   whether to procure.
3. **Widen Channel 1 cheaply** by enriching the identity map from CMC `detail.platforms[]`
   and adding more curated EVM escrows; add non-EVM staking via per-chain explorers if coins
   matter to the result.
4. **Gray-zone classifications** (Entry 18: OP/MNT/MANTA/IMX L2 tokens; RPL/ANKR/SSV/STRD
   LST tokens) can now be revisited with real lock data — OP and RPL/SSV have Snapshot voting
   but no clean security-staking lock, supporting their `other` placement so far.
5. **Confirm N=250** — λ density is governance-token-driven; a tighter screen wouldn't change
   the coin-side gap, which is a source problem, not a universe-size problem.

## 8. Files produced

- `03_data/phase1/asset_onchain_identity.csv` — cmc_id → contract/chain/Snapshot space.
- `03_data/phase1/snapshot_space_map.csv` — 56 cmc_id → Snapshot space (DeFiLlama ∪ curated).
- `03_data/phase1/channel1_eth_staking.csv` — ETH native staking (partial; resumable).
- `03_data/phase1/channel1_evm_locks.csv` — 6 EVM vote-escrow/staking lock ratios (validated).
- `03_data/phase1/channel3_voting.csv` — 55 DAOs' monthly voting turnout.
- `03_data/phase1/lambda_panel.csv` — the λ index (1,308 asset-months) + raw channel values.
- `03_data/phase1/_lambda_channel_diagnostics.csv` — per-month standardizability by channel.
- Raw caches under `03_data/raw/` (defillama, phase1_onchain, snapshot) — gitignored, regenerable.
- `04_code/phase1_*.py` (5 scripts); `04_code/DATA_DECISIONS_LOG.md` Entries 21–27.
